title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: June 26, 2026: Tech Sector Weighs, Industrials Gain Amid Inflation Concerns

items.headline: Mixed Market as Tech Sell-off Continues, Dow Edges Up, Nasdaq Drops
items.summary: U.S. equities saw a mixed close on Thursday, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average advancing, while the Nasdaq Composite fell for the fourth consecutive day, dragged down by a broad sell-off in technology stocks. Investor sentiment remained cautious amid concerns over AI-related valuations and rising chip costs. Pre-market futures for Friday indicate a continued mixed trend, with Nasdaq and S&P 500 futures lower, while Dow futures showed a slight gain.

items.headline: Micron Technology Surges on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Micron Technology ($MU) shares surged 15.7% after reporting robust third-quarter fiscal 2026 earnings and revenue that exceeded analyst expectations, coupled with an upbeat growth forecast. This performance temporarily boosted optimism for semiconductor stocks.

items.headline: Sandisk Rallies Significantly
items.summary: Sandisk ($SNDK) soared 22%, making it one of the day's biggest gainers. The memory chipmaker's rise contributed to the broader semiconductor rally.

items.headline: Apple Drops on Price Hikes and Cost Concerns
items.summary: Apple Inc. ($AAPL) tumbled 6.1% after announcing price increases for its MacBook and iPad models, citing higher chip and component costs. This move raised concerns about the impact of rising memory-chip expenses on profitability.

items.headline: Microsoft Falls After Xbox Price Increases
items.summary: Microsoft Corporation ($MSFT) fell 3.5% after unveiling price hikes for Xbox consoles, adding to investor concerns that companies are passing rising costs on to consumers, impacting demand.

items.headline: Technology Leads Market Lag, Industrials Show Strength
items.summary: Technology stocks were the primary drag on the market, with the Nasdaq Composite falling and the broader tech sector down 0.1%. The Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR (XLY) declined 1.8%, Consumer Staples (XLP) fell 1.1%, and Communication Services (XLC) dropped 1%. In contrast, the Industrials Select Sector SPDR (XLI) advanced 2.2%, with gains in companies like Caterpillar and Deere providing some balance to the market. The Philadelphia SE Semiconductor index (SOX) also managed a 3.2% rise despite broader tech weakness.

items.headline: PCE Inflation Above 4%, Jobless Claims Fall, GDP Revised Upward
items.summary: May's PCE inflation, the Federal Reserve's preferred gauge, climbed above 4% year-on-year, reinforcing expectations for a potential interest rate hike. Core PCE rose 0.3%. Personal income and spending both increased by 0.7%. Initial jobless claims for the week ending June 20 decreased by 12,000 to 215,000, while continuing claims rose by 21,000. The third estimate for first-quarter 2026 GDP was revised upward to a 2.1% increase.

items.headline: Fed Maintains Hawkish Tone as Rate Hike Concerns Linger
items.summary: Policymakers are expected to maintain a hawkish stance due to rising core inflationary pressures and solid labor income. The CME FedWatch Tool indicates a 47% probability of a September rate hike. Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh has emphasized returning inflation to the 2% target as a priority. Traders will monitor speeches from New York Fed President John Williams and Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari.

items.headline: AI-Driven Fatigue Impacts Tech Valuations
items.summary: Technology stocks continued to face 'AI-driven fatigue' as investors grew increasingly cautious about lofty valuations and rising costs within the sector. Concerns over the surging costs of AI data centers and memory chip shortages have led to profit-taking, particularly in Asia, where AI-related stocks saw heavy losses.
